observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to:
Require the City Accountant, in coordination with the City Treasurer in obtaining the relevant reports and documents, to recognize the reconciling items per BRS and adjust the erroneous entries in the SLs;
Make representation with the SP to fast track the resolution on the closure of the bank accounts in LBP – Ozamiz branch to consolidate the funds of the city in the nearer branch; and
Evaluate the current workload of the City Accountant’s Office and consider hiring additional staff, if warranted, to ensure timely submission of mandatory reports.

We recommended to the management to:
Require the City Accountant to prepare journal entry voucher (JEV) to recognize as ‘Accounts Payable’ all unpaid goods and equipment received as at December 31, 2024; and
Enjoin the General Services Office to submit all unpaid purchase orders (P.O.) and Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) for goods and/or equipment received before the end of the year to the City Accountant’s Office prior to the cut-off period of the financial statements to ensure the proper and timely recognition of liabilities.

observations.recommendation:

b. Verify the validity of payables aged two years and more and make payments for those deemed valid. Otherwise, cause the reversion to the General Fund those payables, which had been outstanding for two years or more and with no actual claim, administrative   or judicial, nor covered by perfected contracts on record, in order to present fairly the Accounts Payable account in the financial statements and enable the re-appropriation of additional funds for priority program, projects and activities of the City Government of Tangub.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management to:
Instruct the GSO to conduct physical inventory of supplies. Also, retrieve the SSMIs of the procured inventories from prior periods and for those inventories with unavailable SSMIs, reconstruct the documents if the concerned signatories are still available;
Direct the Accounting Office to provide the GSO of the list of procured inventory items which remained undispensed in the books of accounts and per SSMI’s received from the GSO, prepare JEVs to record inventories issued to appropriate expenditure accounts or prior period adjustments; and
Enjoin the Accounting and the General Services Office to coordinate with each other to conduct periodic reconciliation of their inventory records and prepare JEV on established balances.

We recommended to Management to:
Augment existing manning complements for both General Services and Accounting Offices to aid in addressing the deficiencies noted and in preparation of the carrying out of the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of the city as provided under COA Circular No. 2020-006;
Direct the Inventory Committee to plan/strategize on how to conduct and complete the physical inventory within the prescribed period including the preparation of the PIP. Also, execute the physical count of all PPE and ensure that property records are updated and reconciled with the accounting records based on the results of the said count in order to come up with the correct PPE balances of the city; and
Enjoin all department heads to coordinate with the Accounting and General Services Offices and Inventory Committee to facilitate the conduct of physical count and the reconciliation of the PPE accounts to establish actual existence, ownership and condition of the PPE assigned to their respective department/offices.

We recommended to management to:
Instruct the City Engineer to submit the Certificates of Completion and Acceptance and the Inspection Reports on the completed projects; and
Direct the City Accountant to coordinate with the City Engineer for the immediate submission of the documents required above for all completed projects under the CIP accounts as basis for the preparation of reclassification entries to their appropriate PPE accounts. Completed projects under the Trust Fund shall be transferred to the General Fund upon submission of full liquidation to the grantor. Henceforth, the Accountant shall reclassify projects recorded under the CIP to its appropriate PPE account upon completion of the project.

We recommended to management to direct the City Accountant to:
Analyze the existence of negative SL balances and make the necessary adjusting entries in the books of accounts to correct them; and
Verify the validity of payables aged two years and more and make payments for those deemed valid. Otherwise, cause the reversion to the General Fund those payables, which had been outstanding for two years or more and with no actual claim, administrative or judicial, nor covered by perfected contracts on record, in order to present fairly the Accounts Payable account in the financial statements and enable the re-appropriation of additional funds for priority program, projects and activities of the City Government of Tangub.

We recommended to management to:
Direct the City Accountant to delve into their records to account for the balances from prior years. Return to the personnel concerned the amounts erroneously withheld and remit those which are already due; and
Instruct the HRMO, as the AAO, to regularly update and closely coordinate with the City Accounting and Treasury Offices in ensuring that all contributions and loan amortizations are accurately withheld and remitted within the prescribed periods in order to avoid incurrence of unnecessary penalties as provided under Sections 6(b) and 7 of RA 8291, Section 23(b) of RA 9679 and Section 16(v) of RA 10606.

We recommended to management to direct the City Accounting Office to regularly reconcile the accounting records with that of the lending institution and effect the necessary adjustments in order to reflect the correct balance of the loans payable account.

We recommended to management to:
Enforce the personnel in charge to attach the complete, proper and duly approved driver’s trip tickets to the monthly reports of official travel and fuel consumption before submission to the audit team; and
Enjoin all department heads and prospective passengers to strictly observe all existing rules and regulations on the use of government motor vehicles in order to regulate and minimize unnecessary expenditures for fuel consumption.

We recommended to management to:
Make representation with the City Council to revisit and make necessary amendments on the existing guidelines of the AICS program in order to arrive at a more detailed and enhanced guidelines for each assistance, achieve uniformity in implementation, ease of review and afford equal opportunities for all; and
Direct all the in-charge of the assessment to be keen in screening the requests received and consistently implement the guidelines of the AICS program.

We recommended that the Management provide legal basis for the aforementioned honorarium granted to government officials and employees to determine propriety and reasonableness. Otherwise, refund the amounts received as these are disallowable in audit.

We recommended to management to:
Enjoin the end-user units to keep abreast and comply with DOH issuances particularly on the use of the DPRI as well as other laws, regulations and issuances from various governing boards and offices in the preparation of Purchase Requests or Approved Budget for the Contract in the procurement of essential drugs and medicines; and
Instruct the Accountant to strictly monitor compliance with the said laws and issuances pertaining to the prices of drugs and medicines.

We recommended to management to:
Direct all members of the BAC to be keen in reviewing every detail of the bid documents submitted by the bidders and ensure completeness and propriety by utilizing the checklist provided as guide;
Require the members of the BAC Secretariat to ensure that all applicable documents provided in the checklist are attached in the contracts submitted and heed to communications sent on the deficiencies noted in order to facilitate auditorial and technical review; and
Maintain and be guided by the checklists for all procurement transactions to ensure the completeness of documents for review in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended to managment to:
Direct the GSO head and BAC Secretariat Chairperson to develop strategies to prevent delays in the submission of all POs, contracts and supporting documents for timely legal and auditorial review and inspection; and
Require the BAC to strictly monitor compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 to ensure that all winning bidders have the complete and proper documents.

We recommended to management to:
Exert efforts in the processing of the Certificates of Title of the untitled lots to establish absolute ownership and protect the legal interest of the LGU under Section 148 of COA Circular 92-386;
Make representation and constant coordination with partner national agencies in order to harmonize priority projects. Likewise, establish procedures to strictly enforce timely and adequate planning of investment programming processes on PAPs charged against the city’s 20% LDF to prevent numerous reprogramming of priority projects; and
Submit explanation on the unimplemented projects per current legislative appropriation identified at year-end for them not being pushed-through for use and operation, and furnish plan of action on aforecited projects if still considered priority or be put aside for others of immediate concern.

We recommended to management to:
Instruct the City Accountant to be keen in ensuring that complete and proper supporting documents are attached to the DVs before processing and issuing checks to guarantee that only valid claims will be paid pursuant to existing provisions of law;
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the proper mode of procurement is correctly identified to avoid confusion and to recognize the appropriate supporting documents to require and the proper procedures to be done; and
Require the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the Notice of Award, Purchase Order/Contract of Agreement, and Notice to Proceed, for future procurements through all modes are posted in the PhilGEPS website as required under Section 54.3 of IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended to management to:
Rationalize personnel per approved plantilla for proper matching of work and responsibilities;
Re-evaluate fidelity bond of each AO and be enjoined to apply them as prescribed; and
Instruct the City Treasurer to periodically monitor and raise the noted errors/discrepancies of the generated reports of the E- TRACS to the software provider at the soonest possible time to ensure accurate reports.

We recommended to management to:
Prioritize personnel movement in order to acknowledge the exemplary performance and work attitude of employees by way of promotion to higher rank to allow the entry of new applicants for lower-level positions;
Submit justification on the hiring of all COS workers. Also, instruct these workers to submit their Personal Data Sheets and furnish the audit team; and
Direct the head of the City Human Resource Officer to expedite the selection and hiring processes especially those JO workers with proven work excellence and those considered vital and mandatory positions in compliance with the provisions of the Local Government Code.

We recommended to management to:
Require the GAD focal person to submit the DILG – endorsed GPB in order to assess whether the city’s PAPs addressed the identified specific gender issues contained in the said report. Otherwise, a Notice of Suspension enforcing its submission will be issued for all the GAD disbursements during the year;
Issue an Executive Order constituting the new members of the GFPS. Likewise, capacitate the GFPS by sending them to trainings and seminars offered by authorized agencies or invite GAD experts to conduct the capacity building in order to have knowledge on the proper preparation and implementation of the above-mentioned reports; and
Develop, maintain, and regularly update a GAD database and establish an effective approach in data gathering to be stored in the database and continuously comply with the provisions of Philippine Commission on Women Memorandum Circular No. 2018-04 dated September 19, 2018.

Instruct the General Services Office to conduct physical inventory of supplies. Also, look into their records and ensure submission of the SSMIs to the Accounting Office. In case of unavailability of the SSMI, reconstruct the documents if the concerned signatories are still available;
Direct the Accounting Office to provide the General Services Office of the list of procured inventory items which remained undispensed in the books of accounts; and
Enjoin the Accounting and the General Services Sections to conduct periodic reconciliation   of   their
inventory records and prepare JEV on established balances.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management observe the following provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of the city, viz:
Items 5.0 and 6.0 - General and procedural guidelines in the conduct of physical count of PPEs;
Item 7.0                   -  Disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs;
Item 8.0                   -  Procedures in derecognition of non-existing/missing
PPEs without available record of accountability; and
Item 9.0                   -  Accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to the
Financial Statements.

We have recommended the following:
a. The Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of supplies and materials on the subject dormant inventory accounts and every year thereafter and submit the report as the basis for reconciliation of records.
b.The Management shall request authority from the COA the dropping from the inventory accounts those which amount remained dormant for years if after exhaustion of means in retrieving the records are not availed.
c. The City Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to drop from the books, accounts which had remained dormant in the accounting records and which are no longer available in stock upon receipt of approval from COA.

We have recommended the following:
Reimbursements of cash donations given to individuals will be recommended to be disallowed in audit.
The Management should strictly conform to the guidelines set in DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 11, s.2019 in giving donations/assistance to individuals in 	crisis situation.
Management’s comment:
The Local Chief Executive wanted to expedite the assistance needed by her constituents but have in mind to establish some sort of Petty Cash Fund for immediate assistance to her constituents.

Recommendation:
We have recommended that Management instruct the IT of E-TRACS to revisit the electronic processing on the treasury operations specifically on the accountable forms received and issued, beginning and ending balances to address the aforementioned deficiency.
Management’s comment:
The In-charge of the eTracs operation insisted that the received Official receipt can be found, if it will be coordinated to him since he is well-versed with the system.

We have recommended that the City Mayor requires the City Accountant to cause the timely submission of the paid disbursement vouchers and payrolls and all transaction documents/reports as required within five days after the end of every month.

We have recommended that the City Accountant  make adjusting entry to recognize expense on the consumed and distributed inventories and submit the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) with supporting documents for audit.
We have also recommended that Management  practice sound management of inventory and strictly follow the pertinent provisions of Section 121 of the New Government Accounting System Manual for LGUs, as prescribed under of COA Circular No. 2002-003 dated June 20, 2002.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

Instruct the City Accountant to demand full liquidation of the cash advance granted for COE totalling P30M following the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly Items IV and V thereof. The Accountant shall obligate the grant of cash advance on the basis of the activity budget.

Require the Accountable Officer (AO) to prepare the Report of Disbursements (RD) and submit the same with duly accomplished vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents to the Accountant. For payments based on receipts and invoices only, the AO shall also prepare a liquidation voucher which shall be submitted with the report and supporting documents to the Accountant. He shall ensure that receipt of the report is properly acknowledged by the Accountant. Likewise, he must secure a designation as disbursing officer before obtaining cash advance.

c. Observe PD 1445, particularly Section 91 thereof and the general principles on the disbursements of government funds provided under COA Cir. 97-002, all payment shall be made by checks, except when it is very difficult, impractical and impossible to make payment by check.
observations.managementAction: All liquidations have already been submitted to COA Office.
observations.reason: All liquidations have already been submitted to COA Office.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

Instruct the City Accountant to gather the lacking documentary requirements of          thefund transfer to Philippine Red Cross totaling P500,000.00 following the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

Adhere to the guidelines and documentary requirements on fund transfers to   NGOs provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 especially that no subsequent fund transfer must be granted unless previous fund transfers have been liquidated and required physical status report has been submitted.

Management adhered to the guidelined and documentary requirements on fund transfer.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

Require the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to review these recorded payables to ascertain if these still represent valid claims, otherwise seek authority from the SangguniangPanlungsod to revert these to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund so that these may be made available for funding valuable development programs and other priority activities of the city and that the liabilities would be fairly presented in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The management did as recommended.

We already made a reversal of long outstanding obligations that no longer have valid claims. We will continue to make our assessments on the remaining balances and will make the necessary adjustment if there is a need for the adjustment to be made.
observations.reason: Dormant accounts has been reduced.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

Require the collectors to turn-over/remit all collections daily and intact to the Liquidating Officer in compliance to Sections 26 and 31 of COA Circular No. 92-382, Section 31 (a), Volume I of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and Section 69 of P.D. 1445. 

b. Closely supervise the collecting officers and keep a monitoring of daily collections and remittances.


c. Strengthen internal control over collections and remittances to avoid accumulating huge amounts in the hands of the collector by promulgating and implementing a policy on the turn-over and cut-off of daily collections by the collecting officer to the liquidating officer and imposing administrative sanctions for non-observance thereof.

Management did strengthen their internal control over collections and remittances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to observe the following provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 for thee One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of the city, viz:
Items 5.0 and 6.0 - General and procedural guidelines in the conduct of physical count of PPEs;
Item 7.0                   -  Disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs;
Item 8.0                   -  Procedures in derecognition of non-existing/missing
PPEs without available record of accountability; and
Item 9.0                   -  Accounting entries and disclosure in the Notes to the
Financial Statements.

We recommended the following:
The Inventory Committee to conduct a physical inventory of supplies and materials on the subject dormant inventory accounts and every year thereafter and submit the report as the basis for reconciliation of records.
The management shall request authority from the COA the dropping from the inventory accounts those which amount remained dormant for years if after exhaustion of means in retrieving the records are not availed.
The City Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to drop from the books, accounts which had remained dormant in the accounting records and which are no longer available in stock upon receipt of approval from COA.

We recommended that the City Accountant, City Treasurer and City Social Welfare Development Officer be required to submit the lacking supporting documents/documentary requirements on the payment of Social Amelioration Program (SAP) to beneficiaries amounting to P42,852,000.00.  Henceforth, adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and DSWD, DOLE, DTI, DA, DOF, DBM and DILG JointMemorandum Circular No. 1 Series of 2020.

We recommended that the BAC should strictly observe the pertinent laws, rules, and regulations issued by the GPPB and other regulatory agencies on the procurement at hand for efficient and effective procurements so that the most advantageous prices may be availed and ultimately, wastage of government funds could be avoided.

We recommended to management the following:
Instruct the City Accountant to demand full liquidation of the cash advance granted for COE totalling P30M following the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 97-002 particularly Items IV and V thereof. The Accountant shall obligate the grant of cash advance on the basis of the activity budget.
b.  Require the Accountable Officer (AO) to prepare the Report of Disbursements (RD) and submit the same with duly accomplished vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents to the Accountant. For payments based on receipts and invoices only, the AO shall also prepare a liquidation voucher which shall be submitted with the report and supporting documents to the Accountant. He shall ensure that receipt of the report is properly acknowledged by the Accountant. Likewise, he must secure a designation as disbursing officer before obtaining cash advance.
c.  Observe PD 1445, particularly Section 91 thereof and the general principles on the disbursements of government funds provided under COA Cir. 97-002, all payment shall be made by checks, except when it is very difficult, impractical and impossible to make payment by check.

Recommendations:
Instruct the City Accountant to gather the lacking documentary requirements of          thefund transfer to Philippine Red Cross totaling P500,000.00 following the guidelines set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
Adhere to the guidelines and documentary requirements on fund  	transfers to   NGOs provided in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 especially that no subsequent fund transfer must be granted unless previous fund transfers have been liquidated and required physical status report has been submitted.

Recommendations:
Require the Local Finance Committee to submit monthly report on fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs funded from BGCM and proof of posting of the accumulated reports on the LGUs website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month as required, also an explanation of its failure to submit the same.
Require the City Accountant to explain why special account in the general fund was not created in the recording of the receipt and utilization of the BGCM.
Prepare and submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF for the year 2020 and on the ensuing years using the prescribed format provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002.

Require the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to review these recorded payables to ascertain if these still represent valid claims, otherwise seek authority from the SangguniangPanlungsod to revert these to the Unappropriated Surplus in the General Fund so that these may be made available for funding valuable development programs and other priority activities of the city and that the liabilities would be fairly presented in the financial statements.

In view of the aforementioned deficiencies, we recommended the following:
Require the collectors to turn-over/remit all collections daily and intact to the Liquidating Officer in compliance to Sections 26 and 31 of COA Circular No. 92-382, Section 31 (a), Volume I of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) and Section 69 of P.D. 1445.
Closely supervise the collecting officers and keep a monitoring of daily collections and remittances.
Strengthen internal control over collections and remittances to avoid accumulating huge amounts in the hands of the collector by promulgating and implementing a policy on the turn-over and cut-off of daily collections by the collecting officer to the liquidating officer and imposing administrative sanctions for non-observance thereof.

observations.managementAction:
(a) Additional manpower will be provided to the General Services Office to expedite the conduct of physical count on all the properties of the City. (b) We will reconstitute the Inventory and Appraisal Committee of the City.

The General Services Office has been created last November 27, 2017 by the City Ordinance No. 2017-11-023 and the appropriation has been made in 2018. The item for the General Services Officer has already been published in 2019. However, documents of the applicants have been pending for review by the PSB. For the meantime, an officer has been designated/appointed to facilitate the operations of the Office while the application for the position has been processed.

The General Services Officer will continue to update all the necessary documents simultaneous with the conduct of the physical count.

(a) To capacitate the GSO, inventory committee and other employees concern, they will be sent to trainings and seminar especially those conducted by COA. (b) With the identification of the deficiencies on the operation, the Accountant and the General Services had agreed to streamline the processes to expedite the transactions. A process flow will be created to provide transparency of the operations of the GSO and this will be put into resolution by the Sanggunian. (c) On-going conduct of the inventory for unserviceable properties has been done by the GSO and this will be continued until all unserviceable properties of the City will be identified and properly disposed of.
Insistence that they have legal basis to grant the benefits but failed to submit any.
Management did as advised.
Management did as advised.

The LDRRMO prepared and implement the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the national, regional and provincial framewok.
The prior years’ unspent LDRRM Funds has been incorporated in the current year’s LDRRMFIP.
They are about to implement the pending projects but were not able due to COVID19 Virus. 

Management prepared and submitted a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

Management already sent 2 qualified personnel for sufficient trainings.

Management discontinued issuing temporary receipts.

Management no longer spends collections outright and remitted their collections to the City Treasury.

All collectors were properly bonded.

Budget has  been prepared for the CMO and CCO symbols.
Management voluntarily refunded their travels without the Auditor issuing the Notice of Disallowance.

(a) The matter had been forwarded to the Legal Office for further action to be taken.

(b) The amount will be refunded by the officials through deduction from their honoraria to be claimed.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing observations, we reiterated our prior years’ recommendations as follows:
The inventory committee should (a) strictly conduct actual physical inventory-taking at least annually, (b) prepare a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) using the prescribed format which must be fully filled up; (c) includes all types of PPEs in their inventory; (d) properly place property identification card/property tag to  each item of PPE;
The management should create the position and appoint General Services Officer, a mandatory position in the city government pursuant to Section 490 of RA 7160, to take charge of the office of the general services.
The General Services Officer should (a) make a renewal of ARE every three years; and (b) maintain and update property cards of all properties; and
The management should provide for sufficient GSO manpower and (a) send the GSO, inventory committee and other employees concern to actual trainings and seminars to capacitate them on the keeping, maintenance and disposition of PPE and for proper guidance in property management; (b) properly install strong internal control in safeguarding the government’s properties; and (c) immediately make an inventory of all unserviceable properties and within a reasonable time, cause its disposal.

We recommended the management to submit legal basis on enacting City Ordinance No. 2019-12-031 authorizing the grant of additional bonus to all payees and/or amend certain provision therein changing “additional bonus” to “SRI” pursuant to AO No. 19 should the same be the intent of such ordinance otherwise the whole amount maybe disallowed in audit.

We recommended management to strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 and follow the terms and conditions in the Memorandum of Agreement in order to avoid the accumulation of unliquidated funds transferred to the different barangays.
We further recommended management to:
Send demand letters regularly to all barangays concerned with unliquidated financial assistance requiring the submission of Fund Utilization Report (FUR) with complete supporting documents in order to effectively safeguard the utilization of government funds.
Strictly adhere to COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 in the execution of a Memorandum of Agreement for all fund transfers which shall contain the guidelines and procedures in the implementation of projects by implementing/recipient barangays including their responsibilities in the submission of the liquidation reports.

We recommended management to:
Discontinue the practice of issuing temporary receipts, instead issue Official Receipts for all collections.
Discontinue the practice of spending of collections outright, instead remit all amounts collected to the City Treasurer’s Office from the commencement to the end of the activity.
Properly bond all Collectors being Accountable Officers.
If the CMO and CCO symbols are not included in the contest, prepare a budget for the installation plus the voluntary contribution of the personnel.

We recommended management to observe the rules and regulations prescribed under EO No. 77 on the claims for travel, both local and foreign, particularly on the authorization and documentary requirements. Ensure also that such travels meet the criteria under the said Order to avoid disallowance of claims.

Recommendations:

We recommended the following:

1.1. The Inventory Committee should (a) strictly conduct physical inventory-taking at least annually; (b) prepare a Report on thye Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) using the prescribed format which must be fully filled up; (c) includes all types of PPEs in their inventory; (d) properly lace property identification card/Property tag to each item of PPE.

1.2. The management should create the position and appoint General Service Officer, a mandatory position in the city government pursuant to take charge of the office of the general services.





1.3. The General Services Officer should (a) make a renewal of ARE every three years; and maintain and update property cards of all properties.



1.4. The management should provide for sufficient GSO manpower and (a) send the GSO inventory committee and other employees cncern to actual trainings and seminars to capacitate them on the keeping, maintenance and disposition of PPE and for proper guidance in property management; (b) properly install strong internal control in safeguarding the government’s properties; and (c) immediately make an inventory of all unserviceable properties and within a reasonable time, cause its disposal.

Recommendations:

2.1. The GSO and the Accounting Office should install an adequate system that will ensure the proper and complete recording of its supplies inventory from (a) requisition (b) procurement (c) receipt of deliveries, (d) release to the end-users, down to (e) recording.



2.2. The management should strengthen the capacity of the General Services Office by providing an adequate and capable manpower complement.

Recommendations:

3.1. Require the officials and personnel concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 on the liquidation of cash advances;




3.2. Direct the AOs who are still active employees with dormant accounts to immediately liquidate their cash advances. In case of failure to comply with the directive, cause the withholding of the AOs salaries until accounts are fully settled.



3.3. Adhere to the guidelines and procedures on the request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts and unliquidated cash advances provided in COA Circualr No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to facilitate the request.

Recommendation:

4.1. Prompt submission within the prescribed period of the contracts and other required documents.

Recommendations:

6.1. We recommended the management to require the Philippine Councilor’s League, Vice Mayor’s League of the Philippines and Liga ng mga Barangay sa Pilipinas to issue a government official receipt for all collections made to establish proper financial accountability.

Recommendation:

7.1. We recommended that the whole amount of P793,500.00 paid to all barangay officials as Anniversary bonus be disallowed in audit.

Recommendation:

8.1. We recommended that COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended the following to the Agency:
The inventory committee should (a) strictly conduct actual physical inventory-taking at least annually, (b) prepare a Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) using the prescribed format which must be fully filled up; (c) includes all types of PPEs in their inventory; (d) properly place property identification card/property tag to  each item of PPE;
The management should create the position and appoint General Services Officer, a mandatory position in the city government pursuant to Section 490 of RA 7160, to take charge of the office of the general services.
The General Services Officer should (a) make a renewal of ARE every three years; and (b) maintain and update property cards of all properties; and
The management should provide for sufficient GSO manpower and (a) send the GSO, inventory committee and other employees concern to actual trainings and seminars to capacitate them on the keeping, maintenance and disposition of PPE and for proper guidance in property management; (b) properly install strong internal control in safeguarding the government’s properties; and (c) immediately make an inventory of all unserviceable properties and within a reasonable time, cause its disposal.

We recommended the following:
The GSO and the Accounting Office should install an adequate system that will ensure the proper and complete recording of its supplies inventory from (a) requisition, (b) procurement, (c) receipt of deliveries, (d) release to the end-users, down to (e) recording; and
The management should strengthen the capacity of the general services office by providing an adequate and capable manpower complement.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the officials and personnel concerned to strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and  2012-004 on the liquidation of cash advances;
Direct the AOs who are still active employees with dormant accounts to immediately liquidate their cash advances.  In case of failure to comply with the directive, cause  the withholding of the AOs salaries until accounts are fully settled; and
Adhere to the guidelines and procedures on the request for write-off of dormant receivable accounts and unliquidated cash advances provided in COA Circular No, 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 in order to facilitate the request.

Recommendation:
The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) through its secretariat should observe the submission of contracts and other required documents within the prescribed period under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated 21, 2009.

We recommended the following:
Collect from DL Enterprises liquidated damages for every day of delay at 1/10 of 1% effective November 18, 2018; and
Require DL Enterprises to refund the P1,500,000.00 additional transportation expense, the same being the amount of unnecessary expense incurred by the City of Tangub due to the Enterprise’s failure to complete the project on  time.

Recommendation:
We recommended the management to require the Philippine Councilors’ League, Vice Mayors' League of the Philippines and Liga ng mga Barangay sa Pilipinas to issue a government official receipt for all collections made to establish proper financial accountability.

Recommendation:
We recommended to management to require refund of  the whole amount of P793,500.00 paid to the barangay officials as Anniversary bonus otherwise such payment will finally be disallowed in audit.

Recommendation:
We recommended that COA Circular 2015-008 be strictly followed.

Recommendations:
Prioritize gender mainstreaming efforts in GAD planning and budgeting to be headed by its GAD focal Point system by using existing gender analysis tools such as the HGDG in the identification, design, implementation, management and  monitoring and evaluation stages of the various PAP’s of the city to determine the extent of their gender-responsiveness and amount to be attributed to the GAD budget;
Develop a GAD database or Sex-Disaggregated Data for proper utilization in the planning, budgeting, programming, and policy formulation of the City as well as proper charging of the object of expenditures on actual accomplishments; and
Submit GAD Accomplishment Report for calendar year 2018 for verification on the activities conducted to determine if the gender issues identified are being addressed.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

8.1. We recommended that management should   require the concerned officials to submit immediately the subject reports, thereafter, coordinate with the Accountable Officer, the City Treasurer, the City Budget Officer, the City Accountant and all other concerned to submit promptly the disbursements and collections report together with the paid vouchers, payrolls with its supporting documents and official receipts; and recommend further to suspend the payment of the salaries of the officials responsible for the non-submission of the subject reports until they shall have complied with the requirements.
observations.managementAction: No concrete action

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended the following:

9.1. The management should require the drivers of the motor vehicles to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels supported by trip tickets and attach the documents to the voucher for submission to the COA, through the Administrative Officer for audit purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61;

9.2. The Administrative Officer to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle, together with the Monthly Report of Official Travels, to the Auditor for audit purposes as required under COA Circular No. 77-6 and to maintain a gasoline control card to properly monitor the inventory; and the CGSO/Supply Officer to conduct physical count of the inventories every semester and prepare the inventory report. Reconcile actual inventory on hand and per record for discrepancies noted.

observations.recommendation:
The GSO and the Accounting Office should install an adequate system that will ensure the proper and complete recording of its supplies inventory from (a) requisition, (b) procurement, (c) receipt of deliveries, (d) release to the end-users, down to (e) recording; and
The management should strengthen the capacity of the general services office by providing an adequate and capable manpower complement.

The City Accountant should review and verify the validity of the reconciling items, locate the necessary documents to support the reconciling items and make the necessary adjustments in the books.
The City Accountant should evaluate outstanding checks totalling P779,766, aging six months and above, which are considered stale per Bank Reconciliation Statements as of August 2017;
The necessary adjustments should subsequently be made, in accordance with Section 59 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume 1; and
Henceforth, the City Treasurer should send a written notice to the payee, at least one month before a check becomes stale, reminding him of the existence of the outstanding check.
Direct the City Treasurer and City Accountant to be observant and follow the procedures stipulated in Volumes 41, 42 and 43 of NGAS Manual for Local Government Units.
Require the City Treasurer and City Accountant to reconcile their books of accounts at least in every quarter as provided in  Section 6.3 of COA Circular 97-002.
Require the City Accountant to evaluate the system adopted by the City of Tangub in order to attain the reconciled balances between individual Subsidiary Ledger (SL) and the generated Trial Balance.

Instruct their employees to coordinate with the GSIS for a possible loan restructuring or any other means to rectify the arrearages, and
Direct, not merely advise, its payroll officer and pre-audit section of the accounting department to strictly reflect the correct amount of salary deductions for loan amortizations of the employees-borrowers.
We recommended that the management should strictly adopt the ATM payroll system.

The City should create items for the city veterinarian and city GSO and appoint fund therefor, revise its organizational structure and, within a reasonable period, appoint qualified applicants for the positions.
The LCE should immediately fill up the long line of vacant positions, especially those mandatory offices - veterinarian, GSO, assessor, treasurer, budget officer and LDRRM Officer, so as not to hamper the discharge of the LGU’s governmental functions to its constituents.
Appropriate funds for all unoccupied items under the existing plantilla of position and, within a reasonable time, appoint qualified applicants for the positions.
Hire qualified individuals or regularize the qualified job order and contract of service workers in order to uphold public accountability.

Instruct the CSWDO to (a) prioritize the preparation of payroll for the social pensioners; (b) make representation with the DSWD FO X for the early release of social pension funds for the people of Tangub; and (c) to facilitate the immediate liquidation of the funds received from the DSWD FO X once the amount is utilized.
Direct the accountant to give priority to the pre-audit of payroll for the stipend of senior citizen-beneficiaries;
Direct the disbursing officer to expedite the release of the social pension to the beneficiaries and to promptly submit the liquidation thereof to the Accounting Office.
Recommend to the mayor the possible additions of manpower complement to assist the concerned offices in the preparation, processing, releasing and liquidation of funds in relation to the social pension program of the government.

Recommendations:

We recommended the following:

1.1. Require the management to create General Services Office (GSO) and appoint a General Services Officer;

1.2. Require the Inventory Committee to make a costing on an item for item basis.

1.3. Require the designated Property Officer to:

   a. Maintain property cards to attain reconciled balances with accounting records and secure all assets with property tag numbers and reclassify assets that are worn-out; and

   b. Issue ARE for equip ment issued to officers and employees for proper accountability; and

1.4. Require the Accounting Office to maintin property ledger card for all types of properties in the PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended the following:

10.1. Require the City Government of Tangub to comply with the provisions of Section 313 of RA 7160 which pertains to the maintenance of Special Accounts in the General Fund.


10.2. Require the CAO to maintain complete (SL) for each special account in the General Fund (GF) and generate the corresponding (FS) for the information on the results of operation and to provide basis on decision making of agency top management on whether or not to pursue the venture on such business activities.

10.3. Require the management to enact a resolution or ordinance creating those enterprises/public utilities as an economic entity.
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office maintained Subsidiary Ledger GADTC.


Subsidiary Ledger (SL) was maintained for GADTC.



The LGU created a committee to conduct feasibility study.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

We recommended that management should require the concerned officials to submit subject reports, thereafter, coordinate with the Accountable Officer, the City  Treasurer, the City Budget Officer, the City Accountant and all other concerned to submit promptly the disbursements and collection reports together with the paid vouchers, payrolls with its supporting documents and official receipts; and recommend further to suspend the payment of the salaries of the officials responsible for the non-submission of the subject reports until they shall have complied with the requirements.
observations.managementAction: The same practice as before.
observations.reason: Disbursement and collection reports were not submitted on time.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

 We recommended that Management should require:

1) The drivers of the motor vehicles to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels supported by trip tickets and attach the documents to the voucher for submission to the COA, through the Administrative Officer for audit purposes pursuant to COA Circular No. 77-61;

2) The Adminstrative Officer to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for each motor vehicle, together with the Monthly Report of Official Travels, to the Auditor for audit purposes as required under COA Circular No. 77-61 and to maintain a gasoline control card to properly monitor the inventory; and The CGSI/Supply Officer to conduct physical count of the inventories every semester and prepare the inventory report.  Reconcile actual inventory on hand and per record for discrepancies noted.
observations.managementAction: Did not submit Monthly   Report of Fuel Consumption and Official Travels.
observations.reason: The required reports were not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:
Require the management to create General Services Office (GSO) and appoint a General Services Officer;
Require the Inventory Committee to make a costing on an item for item basis;
Require the designated Property Officer to:
maintain property cards to attain reconciled balances with accounting records and secure all assets with property tag numbers and reclassify assets that are worn-out; and
issue ARE for equipment issued to officers and employees for proper accountability; and
Require the Accounting Office to maintain property ledger card for all types of properties in the PPE accounts.

We recommended to:
Direct the City Accountant to give utmost priority in the preparation of monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.
Direct the City Accountant to closely supervise the accounting personnel tasked to prepare the Monthly Reconciliation Statements.
Require the City Accountant and City Treasurer to conduct monthly reconciliation of the cash accounts to facilitate smooth preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended that the management should stop the practice of charging inappropriate items of expenditures against the 20% development fund, specifically those ones not covered under the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1. Otherwise, the transactions will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the city should (1) transfer both the unspent DRRM-MOOE and QRF in the trust fund books, particularly the P4,140,375 balance of DRRM-MOOE in the years 2012-2015; (2) incorporate in the current year’s LDRRMFIP the prior years’ unspent LDRRM Funds; (3) fully implement all the programs, activities and projects (P/A/Ps) included in the LDRRMFIP, including those unimplemented projects in the years 2012-2015 totaling P12,042,567; (4) revert back to the general fund and be made available to other social services the unutilized 2011 LDRRMF which remained in the books; and (5) prepare and submit a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

We recommended that the City should stop the practice of improperly charging against the Special Education Fund those items that are not considered as allowable programs/activities in accordance with the provisions of Sections 100 (c) and 272 of R.A. No. 7160 and other laws and regulations on the matter. Otherwise, those inappropriate charges will be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the city government, through its Solid Waste Management Board, should prioritize the compliance of the required data for the unqualified and final approval of the SWM Plan.
We recommended that the city government shall (1) make effective plans to address the problem on garbage collection to cover the majority, at the very least, of its component barangays; and (2) adopt measures to promote and ensure the viability and effective implementation of solid waste management programs in its component barangays.
We recommended that the management should enforce the full implementation of the (a) mandatory segregation of solid waste, (b) proper markings of garbage containers, and (c) comply with the requirements for the transport of solid waste as laid down under the provisions of RA 9003.
We recommended that the city should (1) allocate an amount from local solid waste management fund to purchase protective gears and/or equipment for use by the rubbish collectors during gathering and disposal of solid waste; (2) give necessary trainings to the collectors and personnel directly dealing with collection of solid waste; and (3) create a safety committee that would enforce a safety program for the employees especially those directly exposed to danger.
We recommended that the CSWMB faithfully performs its duties and responsibilities on the IEC campaign, particularly the following:
City-wide distribution of IEC campaign materials should be conducted. The materials to be given out should be drafted in a manner where it can be understood by the public.
Develop training modules which should be patterned after those already developed by the Solid Waste Association of the Philippines (SWAP).
Proper coordination between members of the IEC team and the barangay officials should be observed.
Establish focal persons for each barangays who shall be responsible in the arrangements and schedules of IEC campaigns.
Follow-ups on IEC should be conducted regularly in the entire city to ensure that the information and education drive is applied consistently and continuously.
We recommended that Materials Recovery Facility be established in every barangay or cluster of barangays.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management should require the concerned officials to submit immediately the subject reports, thereafter, coordinate with the Accountable Officer, the City Treasurer, the City Budget Officer, the City Accountant and all other concerned to submit promptly the disbursements and collections reports together with the paid vouchers, payrolls with its supporting documents and official receipts; and recommend further to suspend the payment of the salaries of the officials responsible for the non-submission of the subject reports until they shall have complied with the requirements.

We recommended the following to the management:
Require the City government of Tangub to comply with the provisions of Section 313 of RA 7160 which pertains to the maintenance of Special Accounts in the General Fund.
Require the CAO to maintain complete (SL) for each special account in the general fund (GF) and generate the corresponding (FS) for the information on the results of operation and to provide basis on decision making of agency top management on whether or not to pursue the venture on such business activities.
Require the management to enact a resolution or ordinance creating those enterprises/public utilities as an economic entity.

Recommendations:

1.	Create General Services Office and appoint a General Services Officer;

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

1. The City Government comply with the provisions of Section 313 of RA 7160 which pertains to the maintenance of Special Accounts in the GF; and

2. The CAO maintain complete SL for each special account in the GF and generate the corresponding FS, for the information on the results of operation and to provide basis on decision making of agency top management on whether or not to pursue the venture on such business activities.
observations.managementAction: Initial actions were already made.
observations.reason: There were numerous requirements by the DBM in the setting up of an economic enterprise and the same cannot be complied with by the City rightaway. However, the preparation of a feasibility study as one of the requirements is already underway.
